reforms.description: The government established 20 study groups to review spending and fiscal expenditures, focusing on areas including innovation and applied research to identify potential structural savings.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: While aimed at improving efficiency, the impact on growth is uncertain as it may lead to cuts in innovation funding.
reforms.status_evidence: The groups presented their results on April 1st, 2010.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of study groups is a procedural reform aimed at identifying savings rather than a direct innovation policy change.
reforms.source_quote: The government established 20 study groups in key policy areas to review spending and fiscal expenditures in its each of the areas.

reforms.description: The Dutch government proposed an innovative road pricing scheme aimed at reducing congestion and environmental impacts by implementing a GPS-based per-kilometre charge for all vehicles, which is expected to be fully operational by 2017. This pioneering scheme is designed to charge users for each kilometre driven, aligning with environmental objectives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The road pricing scheme is expected to reduce congestion, which can enhance productivity and efficiency in transportation, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposal for the road pricing scheme has been officially announced by the government.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in transportation policy aimed at environmental sustainability.
reforms.source_quote: The cabinet approved the road pricing scheme in mid November 2009, and the scheme was expected to pass Parliament during spring 2010.

reforms.description: The government introduced a reduced working hour programme to support firms facing temporary declines in activity, allowing them to maintain employment while reducing labor costs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By maintaining employment during downturns, the programme helps preserve human capital and supports firms' recovery, contributing to overall economic stability.
reforms.status_evidence: The reduced working hour programme has been successfully implemented to support firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is significant as it addresses employment challenges during economic downturns but does not represent a structural change.
reforms.source_quote: The government-sponsored reduced working hour programme has lowered the cost of labour hoarding by allowing companies with significant and temporary declines in activity to put partly redundant workers on unemployment benefits for the hours they do not work.

reforms.description: The government introduced a broader R&D tax rebate to provide liquidity to companies during the economic crisis, aimed at stimulating innovation and research activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The R&D tax rebate is designed to stimulate innovation and research activities, which can enhance productivity and drive economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The broader R&D tax rebate has been successfully implemented to support companies during the economic crisis.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is significant as it expands existing tax incentives to support R&D during a crisis, but it does not represent a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The measures... included a broader R&D tax rebate, abolishing the air flight tax, energy investment tax credit, various tax cuts for SMEs.
